title: helianthemum pergamaceum var. obtusatum
intro: Helianthemum pergamaceum var. obtusatum, also known as Rock Rose or Fringed Rockrose, is a flowering plant in the Cistaceae family, native to the Mediterranean region and parts of Europe. Traditionally, Rock Rose has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, attributed to its high concentrations of flavonoids and saponins. The plant is believed to provide several health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving wound healing, as well as exhibiting potential anti-cancer and cardiovascular-protective effects. Rock Rose can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and has also been used in traditional medicine to treat various conditions, including skin irritations, digestive issues, and respiratory problems.
